name: accdb_compaction_completed
description: A single accounts-database partition finished compacting. Compaction scans one partition, relocates its still-live account records forward into the next storage tier, and reclaims the partition. Emitted once per partition per compaction pass by the accdb tile. Partitions are large (e.g. 32 GiB) so this is a rare, coarse-grained event rather than a hot-path counter.

partition_idx.description: Index of the partition that was compacted, identifying its slot in the accdb partition pool.

src_layer.description: Storage tier the partition was compacted from. Layer 0 is the hot tier that takes live execution writes; higher layers are progressively colder. Records are promoted one tier per compaction.

dest_layer.description: Storage tier the surviving live records were relocated into, normally src_layer+1, clamped at the coldest tier.

bytes_scanned.description: Total bytes of account records walked in the partition this pass, live and dead combined. Equals the partition's written extent at the time compaction started.

bytes_freed.description: Dead bytes in the partition that triggered compaction: space held by account records that had been superseded by newer versions. This is the space compaction reclaims.

accounts_relocated.description: Number of still-live account records copied forward into dest_layer this pass.

bytes_relocated.description: Total bytes of live account records copied forward into dest_layer this pass. Compare to bytes_scanned for the live fraction of the partition.

dead_records.description: Number of records skipped because no live index entry referenced them, i.e. records that were already superseded. Together with accounts_relocated this accounts for every record scanned.

start_time.description: Wall-clock time at which this compaction pass began, in nanoseconds since the Unix epoch.

end_time.description: Wall-clock time at which this compaction pass completed, in nanoseconds since the Unix epoch. Subtract start_time for the pass duration.
